evals.expected_output: A structured PRD review report written to .lyra/prd-reviews/EXPAND-411-review.md. The review should cover: scope quality across PM dimensions (strategic intent, edge cases, gaps, RBAC, feature availability), technical concerns (Kafka state risk, schema changes, GDPR deactivation blocker, SSO token security, iOS resource gap), a scored 4-pillar scorecard, and prioritised open questions. Findings should be grounded in the PRD content, not generic advice.

assertions.description: Evidence that the Jira ticket EXPAND-411 was fetched (ticket title or content referenced in the report, not just the ID)
Report is written to .lyra/prd-reviews/EXPAND-411-review.md (file exists and is non-empty)
Report includes a 4-pillar scorecard (Strategic Intent, User Experience, Execution Clarity, Technical Guardrails) with numeric scores
Report flags the deactivation No-Go as a GDPR/compliance risk, not merely a scope deferral
Report identifies the Kafka-as-state-store risk (distributed state, out-of-order events, or reconciliation gap)
Report flags the contradiction between App-bar iOS scope and the absence of an iOS engineer
Report includes an Open Questions section with at least 3 prioritised questions
Findings cite specific areas of the PRD (not generic advice) — at least one finding references a named requirement, field, or assumption from the ticket
Scope review flags that requirements are organised by technical layer (backend/frontend/apps) rather than by user domain or behaviour
Scope review notes the absence of MoSCoW or equivalent prioritisation scheme

Technical review evaluates the ISMS section for both correctness (does the design satisfy 'customer must not access EonX when not validated'?) and completeness (what is missing — e.g. private key handling for JWT signing, token revocation, PII data flow to EonX)
